DECREES.Title: Land Inactivity Resolution
DECREES.Description: A decree establishing a tiered system for handling inactive land ownership to ensure economic productivity while respecting property rights.
DECREES.Content:
The Council of Ten, concerned with the economic stagnation caused by inactive land ownership, hereby establishes the following tiered system:

1. During the first 7 days of owner inactivity, all building rights remain exclusively with the land owner.

2. After 7 days and until 30 days of inactivity, any citizen may construct buildings on the inactive land upon payment of a reasonable fee to the owner's account. Such construction shall be limited to temporary structures.

3. After 30 days of continued inactivity, the land shall be subject to a Dutch auction, beginning at twice the market value and decreasing daily until purchased, with proceeds transferred to the original owner's account.

This decree serves to balance the sacred right of property ownership with the Republic's need for continued economic development.

Effects.Description: Days before land becomes available for temporary building
Days before inactive land enters Dutch auction
Type of buildings allowed during the 7-30 day period

DECREES.Comments: Implemented to address growing concerns about economic stagnation in certain districts due to inactive landowners.
